evals.name: flow-a-fresh-settlement
evals.prompt: I need a gap analysis HTML report for the WS6 Settlement workflow. Compare the BPMN file at output/process/BPMN/WS6-02_Settlement.bpmn against the rebate-accounting / settlement requirements in input/1_Documents Received/Detailed_Requirements_FINAL.md (focus on FR_M20–M28 and any FR_M references that the BPMN tasks cite). Cross-reference RFP §3.1 settlement scope at input/1_Documents Received/RFP.md. The glossary is at output/requirement/ubiquitous-language.md. Use the project's existing gap-analysis house style — the file should be self-contained HTML with theme switching (system / light / dark), severity-tagged gap table, and the standard architecture summary at the bottom. Save the report and tell me the path when you're done.
evals.expected_output: A self-contained HTML file modelled on the existing reports in output/requirement/gap-analysis/. Must include theme toggle, BPMN-aligned phase rows with cards, gap-register table with severity classes, and architecture summary. New gap IDs since this is a fresh report.

assertions.text: Output is a self-contained HTML file with <!DOCTYPE html>
Title contains 'Settlement'
Theme toggle present: .theme-toggle div with 3 buttons (system/light/dark)
Theme JS present: setTheme() and applyTheme() functions, plus gap-analysis-theme localStorage key
Light theme CSS variables defined: [data-theme="light"] block
Phase-flow card system used: at least one card-inscope AND at least one card-gap or card-implied
Gap register table has Severity and Workstream column headers
At least 5 gap rows produced (substantive analysis, not a stub)
Severity classes applied: sev-critical, sev-high, or sev-medium tagged on rows
Workstream pill class used (pill-ws*)
Architecture summary has all 3 boxes: box-confirmed, box-implied, box-gap
References at least one FR_M2x identifier (engagement with source material)
Provenance: cites the BPMN file or task IDs in the gap descriptions

evals.name: flow-a-update-approval
evals.prompt: Update the existing approval workflow gap analysis report at output/requirement/gap-analysis/approval-workflow-gap-analysis.html. Add one new finding: the WS4-01 / WS4-02 / WS4-03 BPMN files have no explicit error-handling sub-processes for SAP integration calls routed via JARVIS (referenced in FR_W08). Treat this as a High severity Vendor-owned BPMN modelling gap. The glossary is at output/requirement/ubiquitous-language.md. Preserve all existing gap IDs and the architecture summary boxes; only add the new row in the gap-register table, refresh the date in the subtitle to 2026-04-30, and add a corresponding bullet under "Missing — Must Be Raised". Save the updated file and tell me the path when you're done.
evals.expected_output: An updated HTML file that preserves all existing gap IDs, adds one new High-severity row with the next available gap ID, updates the date in the subtitle, and adds a matching bullet to the box-gap arch box.

assertions.text: Output file exists and is valid HTML
Subtitle date updated to 2026-04-30
All original gap IDs preserved (count and identity match the source file)
Exactly one new gap row added with severity class sev-high
New row references FR_W08 and JARVIS/SAP error-handling theme
New gap ID uses an existing prefix (BPM- or G-) and the next available number
box-gap arch box has one new <li> bullet referencing the new gap ID
box-confirmed and box-implied bullet lists are unchanged from the source
Theme toggle and theme JS preserved intact
File size is within ±20% of the source file size (no accidental truncation)

evals.name: flow-b-regenerate-register
evals.prompt: Regenerate the master gap register HTML at output/requirement/gap-analysis/gap-register.html by re-reading every individual gap-analysis HTML file in output/requirement/gap-analysis/ (skip gap-register.html itself). The glossary is at output/requirement/ubiquitous-language.md. Re-extract every gap row, regroup them into the seven canonical categories used by the existing register, recompute the scorecard counts and ID lists, and update the last-updated date in the subtitle to 2026-04-30. The data-sev and data-ws attributes on every <tr> must match the filter buttons — verify before saving. Save the regenerated file and tell me the path when you're done.
evals.expected_output: A regenerated gap-register.html with scorecard count = total gap rows, all rows carrying data-sev and data-ws attributes that match the filter button strings, seven categories with category headers, theme toggle, and filter JS preserved.

assertions.text: Output file exists and is valid HTML
H1 contains 'Master Gap' and 'Register'
Subtitle date updated to 2026-04-30
Five scorecard tiles present: tile-critical, tile-high, tile-medium, tile-open, tile-resolved
Filter bar present with 13 filter buttons (all/critical/high/medium/open/resolved/ws6/ws5/ws1/arch/cross/reg/comm)
filterRows() JS function present
setTheme() JS function and gap-analysis-theme localStorage key present
At least 5 category headers (category-header divs)
Every tbody <tr> has both data-sev and data-ws attributes
data-sev values are all in {critical, high, medium, low, open, resolved}
data-ws values are all in {ws1, ws2, ws3, ws4, ws5, ws6, ws7, arch, cross, reg, comm}
Sum of scorecard counts equals total tbody <tr> count (within ±3)
Owner pills used: at least one owner-bsc-sap, owner-bsc-biz or owner-vendor
Severity badges used: <span class="sev sev-critical"> badge form (not bare class on td)
